chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-778 of 2026 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Misc. No.3246/2026. 2. For orders on Office Objection No.1. 3. For orders on Misc. No.3247/2026. 4. For orders on Misc. No.3248/2026. 5. For hearing of main case. 10.02.2026. Ms. Minaal Tariq, Advocate for the Petitioner. ------ 1. Urgent Application is allowed. 2-5. The Petition has been preferred by the Privatization Commission of Pakistan, impugning a Show Cause Notice dated 22.01.2026, emanating from the Wafaqi Mohtasib (Ombudsman)’s Secretariat, alleging non- compliance of the Order dated 28.10.2025 made by the President of Pakistan on the Representation filed by the Respondent No.2 against the Order of Wafaqi Mohtasib (Ombudsman)’s Secretariat in Complaint No.WMS-HQR/0009606/2024, and calling upon the Secretary of the Commission to identify the person(s) who have acted in defiance thereof so that a Special Report in the matter may be laid before the President accordingly. A perusal of the Petition reflects that the President’s Order is already under challenge before the Islamabad High Court in Writ Petition No.200/2026 preferred by the Commission wherein an interim Order was apparently made on 16.01.2026 suspending its operation, and the pendency of that matter and subsistence of the interim Order is the only ground raised in the Memo of Petition in relation to the Show Cause Notice, with all other grounds relating instead to the merits of the 2 President’s Order. Needless to say, that is not the subject of the challenge before us and we would consciously refrain from touching on such aspect.

A perusal of the Petition reflects that the President’s Order is already under challenge before the Islamabad High Court in Writ Petition No.200/2026 preferred by the Commission wherein an interim Order was apparently made on 16.01.2026 suspending its operation, and the pendency of that matter and subsistence of the interim Order is the only ground raised in the Memo of Petition in relation to the Show Cause Notice, with all other grounds relating instead to the merits of the 2 President’s Order. Needless to say, that is not the subject of the challenge before us and we would consciously refrain from touching on such aspect. As matters stand, in our view, the Show Cause Notice in question does not of itself give rise to any actionable grievance and the Petitioner may reply thereto so as to point out that its Petition before the Islamabad High Court remains pending and that the interim Order remains in the field. If any definite action is then taken that runs contrary to that Order, the Petitioner may agitate the matter before the same Court, rather than seeking piecemeal adjudication through different Forums. The Petition stands dismissed accordingly, alongwith the pending Miscellaneous Applications. JUDGE JUDGE MUBASHIR
